Patch Set 3:

The initial mount failed in lustre-initialization-1 right after reformat with:

 mount.lustre: mount /dev/mapper/lvm--Role_MDS-P1 at /mnt/mds1 failed: Operation already in progress
 The target service is already running. (/dev/mapper/lvm--Role_MDS-P1)

I'm not sure if that is because the test environment was having problems, or because of a defect in the patch.  I've resubmitted the patch for testing, but if it fails again then there is clearly a problem with the patch.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(15 comments)

I like this patch in general, and I think it is important to clean up this old code, but I think that splitting the device refcounting across multiple files is a mistake that will just lead to added complexity later.  Better to keep this all in one place.

Patch Set 7:

> -1 until I understand why obd_dev[s]_lock cannot be static in genops.c and have obd_num2obd() return a refcount on the device (and fix up any places that do not drop the reference).

because it is will be very huge patch and it's different problem. We have a two problems - incorrect cleanup and wrong style to account an refcounting - in huge number of places we don't take an reference to OBD. It's very huge patch will be. and it's very easy complete with replace obddevs to hash.

it's same story as ldlm cleanup - i started it with 10kb patch, but after you suggestions it's 400kb patch with huge changes on LDLM code. and i stop work on it due problem of supporting such patch. Now you want it story again. I really don't understand why we need resolve different problem in same patch.

(23 comments)

It is great that you addressed my comments about moving obd_dev_lock into a single file.  That could be done in a later patch.

There are still a number of other issues that were brought up in previous inspections that should be addressed (preferably fixed, but at least commented on) rather than being ignored.

Patch Set 13: Verified-1 Code-Review-1

This patch is causing failures in MDS unmounting:

 Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: umount -d -f /mnt/ost1
 Lustre: lustre-OST0000 is waiting for obd_unlinked_exports more than 8 seconds. The obd refcount = 8. Is it stuck?
 LustreError: 6631:0:(genops.c:1741:obd_exports_barrier()) LBUG
 Pid: 6631, comm: umount

 Call Trace:
 libcfs_debug_dumpstack+0x55/0x80 [libcfs]
 lbug_with_loc+0x47/0xb0 [libcfs]
 obd_exports_barrier+0x156/0x190 [obdclass]
 ofd_device_fini+0x5f/0x260 [ofd]
 class_cleanup+0x552/0xd10 [obdclass]
 class_process_config+0x1fea/0x27c0 [obdclass]
 class_manual_cleanup+0x121/0x870 [obdclass]
 server_put_super+0xb37/0xe50 [obdclass]
 generic_shutdown_super+0x5b/0xe0
 kill_anon_super+0x16/0x60
 lustre_kill_super+0x36/0x60 [obdclass]
 deactivate_super+0x57/0x80
 mntput_no_expire+0xbf/0x110
 sys_umount+0x7b/0x3a0

Patch Set 13:

Andreas,

that panic don't caused that patch if you don't rebase patch to last master by hand. That panic caused bug in hashed function for nodemap - aka LU-5963.
let me rebase and resend.

Patch Set 23:

> As for the if (blah == NULL) the upstream developers don't like that but prefer if (!blah). Once I merged the style changes from upstream into master you will see a lot of changes of the if (!blah) and if (blah) format.

parents.subject: LU-9990 lnet: add backwards compatibility for YAML config

commit.subject: LU-4134 obdclass: obd_device improvement
commit.message:
LU-4134 obdclass: obd_device improvement

The patch removes self exports from obd's reference counting which
allows to avoid freeing of self exports by zombie thread.
A pair of functions class_register_device()/class_unregister_device()
is to make sure that an obd can not be referenced again once its
refcount reached 0. For target_handle_connect() take a reference for
obd_device during finding it by name.

Fix grant mismatch message
	"tot_granted 4194304 != fo_tot_granted".
